term_inventory_greek.meaning: contest, violent struggle; agony, anguish, anxiety Lk. 22:44
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: agōn-ia, he, contest, struggle for victory; gymnastic exercise; of the mind, agony, anguish, en phobō kai pollē agōnia

term_inventory_greek.meaning: to grieve, be anxious, in agony anguished; to pain, either bodily or mentally; passive to be in an agony, be tormented, be distressed Lk. 2:48; 16:24, 25; to be distressed, grieved, Acts 20:38
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: odyn-aō, aorist odunēsai: passive, cause one pain or suffering; passive, feel pain, suffer pain; Ionic dialect present odyneomai

term_inventory_greek.meaning: labor, birth pain; agony (of death); the spasms, or pains, of a woman in travail, a birth pang, 1Thess. 5:3; plural metaphorically birth throes, preliminary troubles to the development of a catastrophe, Mt. 24:8; Mk. 13:8; from the Hebrew, a stringent band, a snare, noose, Acts 2:24
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: odis, inos, he: mostly in plural, pangs or throes of childbirth; in singular also, that which is born amid throes, child; metaphorically, travail, anguish; fruit of the mind's travail; odindes thanatou, the bonds of death (due to confusion of Heb. hebel 'pang' with hebel 'cord')

term_inventory_greek.meaning: to torture, torment; (passive) to be tortured, tormented, in pain; primarily to apply the lapis Lydius or touchstone; metaphorically to examine, scrutinize, try, either by words or torture; in NT to afflict, torment; passive to be afflicted, tormented, pained, by diseases, Mt. 8:6, 29, 35; to be tossed, agitated, as by the waves, Mt. 14:24
term_inventory_greek.lsj_entry: basanizō, rub upon the touch-stone; put to the test, prove; investigate scientifically; of persons, examine closely, cross-question; question by applying torture, torture, rack; passive to be put to the torture; to be tortured by disease; metaphorically of style, strain

verse_records.verse_text: And being in agony he prayed more earnestly; and his sweat became like great drops of blood falling down to the ground.
This would be my comfort; I would even exult in pain unsparing, for I have not denied the words of the Holy One.
The peoples have heard; they tremble; pangs have seized the inhabitants of Philistia.
Trembling took hold of them there, anguish as of a woman in labor.
We have heard the report of it; our hands fall helpless; anguish has taken hold of us, pain as of a woman in labor.
O inhabitant of Lebanon, nested among the cedars, how you will be pitied when pangs come upon you, pain as of a woman in labor!
The king of Babylon heard the report of them, and his hands fell helpless; anguish seized him, pain as of a woman in labor.
Now why do you cry aloud? Is there no king in you? Has your counselor perished, that pain seized you like a woman in labor?
And he said to me, 'Stand beside me and kill me, for anguish has seized me, and yet my life still lingers.'
And when his parents saw him, they were astonished. And his mother said to him, 'Son, why have you treated us so? Behold, your father and I have been searching for you in great distress.'
And he called out, 'Father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus to dip the end of his finger in water and cool my tongue, for I am in anguish in this flame.'
But Abraham said, 'Child, remember that you in your lifetime received your good things, and Lazarus in like manner bad things; but now he is comforted here, and you are in anguish.'
being sorrowful most of all because of the word he had spoken, that they would not see his face again. And they accompanied him to the ship.

And the next generation, your children who rise up after you, and the foreigner who comes from a far land, will say, when they see the afflictions of that land and the sicknesses with which the Lord has made it sick—
In the course of time, at the end of two years, his bowels came out because of the disease, and he died in great agony. His people made no fire in his honor, like the fires made for his fathers.
who forgives all your iniquity, who heals all your diseases,

If I go out into the field, behold, those pierced by the sword! And if I enter the city, behold, the diseases of famine! For both prophet and priest ply their trade through the land and have no knowledge.'

They shall die of deadly diseases. They shall not be lamented, nor shall they be buried. They shall be as dung on the surface of the ground. They shall perish by the sword and by famine, and their dead bodies shall be food for the birds of the air and for the beasts of the earth.
All these are but the beginning of the birth pains.
For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom. There will be earthquakes in various places; there will be famines. These are but the beginning of the birth pains.
God raised him up, loosing the pangs of death, because it was not possible for him to be held by it.
While people are saying, 'There is peace and security,' then sudden destruction will come upon them as labor pains come upon a pregnant woman, and they will not escape.
How often is it that the lamp of the wicked is put out? That their calamity comes upon them? That God distributes pains in his anger?
when they crouch, bring forth their offspring, and are delivered of their young?
What will you say when they set as head over you those whom you yourself have taught to be friends to you? Will not pangs take hold of you like those of a woman in labor?
Damascus has become feeble, she turned to flee, and panic seized her; anguish and sorrows have taken hold of her, as of a woman in labor.
The pangs of childbirth come for him, but he is an unwise son, for at the right time he does not present himself at the opening of the womb.
And all the men of Israel rose up out of their place and set themselves in array at Baal-tamar, and the men of Israel who were in ambush rushed out of their place from Maareh-geba.
Or who shut in the sea with doors when it burst out from the womb,
Behold, if the river is turbulent he is not frightened; he is confident though Jordan rushes against his mouth.
Yet you are he who took me from the womb; you made me trust you at my mother's breasts.

Son of man, raise a lamentation over Pharaoh king of Egypt and say to him: You consider yourself a lion of the nations, but you are like a dragon in the seas; you burst forth in your rivers, trouble the waters with your feet, and foul their rivers.
This day I will begin to put the dread and fear of you on the peoples who are under the whole heaven, who shall hear the report of you and shall tremble and be in anguish because of you.
The battle pressed hard against Saul, and the archers found him, and he was badly wounded by the archers.
The battle pressed hard against Saul, and the archers found him, and he was wounded by the archers.
The wicked man writhes in pain all his days, through all the years that are laid up for the ruthless.
When the waters saw you, O God, when the waters saw you, they were afraid; indeed, the deep trembled.
They will be dismayed: pangs and agony will seize them; they will be in anguish like a woman in labor. They will look aghast at one another; their faces will be aflame.
Be ashamed, O Sidon, for the sea has spoken, the stronghold of the sea, saying: I have neither labored nor given birth, I have neither reared young men nor brought up young women.
When the report comes to Egypt, they will be in anguish over the report about Tyre.
Like a pregnant woman who writhes and cries out in her pangs when she is near to giving birth, so were we because of you, O Lord;
we were pregnant, we writhed, but we have given birth to wind. We have accomplished no deliverance in the earth, and the inhabitants of the world have not fallen.
Woe to him who says to a father, 'What are you begetting?' or to a woman, 'With what are you in labor?'

Sing, O barren one, who did not bear; break forth into singing and cry aloud, you who have not been in labor! For the children of the desolate one will be more than the children of her who is married, says the Lord.
Before she was in labor she gave birth; before her pain came upon her she delivered a son.
Who has heard such a thing? Who has seen such things? Shall a land be born in one day? Shall a nation be brought forth in one moment? For as soon as Zion was in labor she brought forth her children.

O Lord, do not your eyes look for truth? You have struck them down, but they felt no anguish; you have consumed them, but they refused to take correction. They have made their faces harder than rock; they have refused to repent.
The land trembles and writhes in pain, for the Lord's purposes against Babylon stand, to make the land of Babylon a desolation, without inhabitant.
And I will set fire to Egypt; Pelusium shall be in great agony; Thebes shall be breached, and Memphis shall face enemies by day.
Before them peoples are in anguish; all faces grow pale.

Writhe and groan, O daughter of Zion, like a woman in labor, for now you shall go out from the city and dwell in the open country; you shall go to Babylon. There you shall be rescued; there the Lord will redeem you from the hand of your enemies.
Ashkelon shall see it, and be afraid; Gaza too, and shall writhe in anguish; Ekron also, because its hopes are confounded. The king shall perish from Gaza; Ashkelon shall be uninhabited;
Lord, my servant is lying paralyzed at home, suffering terribly.
but the boat by this time was a long way from the land, beaten by the waves, for the wind was against them.
And he saw that they were making headway painfully, for the wind was against them. And about the fourth watch of the night he came to them, walking on the sea. He meant to pass by them,
She was pregnant and was crying out in birth pains and the agony of giving birth.

cross_registry_links.note: chal.cha.lah appears in the related words clusters for chi.lah, chil, and chul in this file, and was listed in the header as a primary Hebrew term for agony (chalchalah). It is a known term in the anguish word set.
chul (H2342I) is the root verb underlying chi.lah (H2427B), chil (H2427A), and chal.cha.lah. This root family bridges agony and anguish word sets.
odunaō (G3600) carries grief/distress alongside agony in its meaning range. Acts 20:38 (grieved) may overlap with grief word set.

basanos (G0928 noun form) is listed in the header as a primary Greek term for agony and appears in the related words cluster for basanizō. The noun form does not have its own word analysis block in this source file.

data_quality_flags.description: The file header lists 'chalchalah' (chal.cha.lah) as a primary Hebrew term for agony (Isa 21:3, Nah 2:10, Ezek 30:4). This term does not have a word analysis block in the STEP section. It appears only in related words clusters for other terms. No term_id can be assigned. Verses Isa 21:3, Nah 2:10, Ezek 30:4 are absent from the source file.

The file header lists 'basanos' (noun, G0928 noun) as a primary Greek term for agony (Matt 4:24, Luke 16:23). The STEP section contains a word analysis block only for basanizō (verb form, G0928H). The noun basanos has no independent word analysis block. Matt 4:24 and Luke 16:23 do not appear in the verse records for basanizō and are absent from the source file entirely.

ta.cha.lu.im (H8463) is marked 'Exclude word / This word only' in the STEP term list, indicating the source file explicitly flags this term as outside the agony semantic range. Its meaning is 'diseases' with no direct agony sense. Recorded here per specification but flagged for exclusion review in Session B. The verse 2Ch 21:19 uses agony in the translation but the term itself means physical disease, not the inner-being state of agony.

chil (H2427A) and chi.lah (H2427B) share identical meaning blocks, identical occurrence counts (about 7x), the same root family (CHUL), and an identical related words list. The source file itself notes under chil that it 'also means chi.lah (H2427B).' These appear to be masculine and feminine noun forms of a single lexical item. Consolidation to a single entry under a shared H2427 grouping should be considered in Session B.

gi.ach (H1518) means 'to burst forth' — a physical action verb. None of its verses in the source file depict agony as an inner-being state. The connection to agony appears to be via the birthing metaphor (bursting from the womb) rather than an emotional or soulish state. Its inclusion in the agony word set should be reviewed in Session B.

The word analysis block for basanizō opens with the gloss line ': anguish' on its own line before the full meaning block, suggesting a partial format artefact in the source file. The full meaning block follows correctly. No data loss, but the format is irregular.

chul (H2342I) has about 56 occurrences but the source file supplies only 19 verse references. The majority of occurrences are not represented in the verse list. This is expected for high-frequency verbs in STEP exports but is flagged for awareness.
